message:
perf(list): add parallelism threshold and use proper-case provider names

Two improvements to the list command:

1. Introduce LIST_PARSE_PARALLEL_THRESHOLD (256) to avoid rayon
   overhead for small session lists. When a provider has fewer than
   256 candidate sessions, use a sequential iterator instead of
   into_par_iter(). Rayon's thread pool setup and work-stealing
   synchronization cost more than they save for small collections,
   so this improves latency for the common case where most providers
   have only a handful of sessions.

2. Switch provider_display() from returning lowercase slugs (e.g.,
   "claude-code") to returning proper-cased display names (e.g.,
   "Claude Code", "ChatGPT", "OpenCode"). The grouped list output
   heading was previously calling .to_uppercase() on the slug, which
   produced awkward results like "CLAUDE-CODE". Now the heading uses
   the proper-cased name directly without .to_uppercase(), producing
   cleaner output like "[bold]Claude Code[/]: 3 session(s)".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
